supersedes.reason: The earlier run of the same date hit its search budget before finishing. Three subdimensions were scored on stale or absent evidence (EQ3 on 2020-2022 COVID-era data, B5 on absence of evidence, A5 on an untested capability). All three were re-researched against primary sources. The re-run also found Rx Kids, a $270 million prenatal and infant cash program the prior run missed entirely, which moved four subdimension scores.

dimensions_changed.driver: EQ3 re-researched onto MDHHS disparity data published 18 June 2025; Rx Kids evidence added to EQ1 and EQ5.
B5 corrected from 2 to 3. See bnd_correction below.
Rx Kids outcome evidence added to S1, S3 and S5.

rank_note: Rank intentionally null. site/src/data/indexes/us-states.json contains only 21 of 51 jurisdictions; ranks 9-38 were lost in the original legacy-HTML extraction and survivors were renumbered contiguously, so the displayed rank column is not national rank. Michigan does not currently appear in the published index at all and must not be assigned a rank until the index is rebuilt with all 51 jurisdictions.
calibration_note: Not calibrated to published us-states rank order (known corrupt). Calibrated to methodology anchors and to the states assessed at full depth on 2026-07-19: Georgia 26.9, Iowa 30.6, Ohio 38.1, Delaware 53.8, Pennsylvania 54.4, New York 56.9. Composite verified with computeCompositeFromDimensions in site/scripts/lib/scoring.mjs: dimension mean 3.15, base composite 53.75, stdDev 0.37 (consistencyMult 1.0), but all 8 of 8 dimensions fall below 4.0 so weaknessFactor is 0 and integrationPremium is 0. Final composite 53.8. Michigan is evenly balanced and earns no integration premium, because the methodology only pays a consistency bonus when consistency is paired with strength somewhere. Michigan has no dimension above 3.6.

reasons: One fact was penalised twice. The prior run made the Michigan FOIA exemption the primary evidence for B3 (Scope Clarity) and a primary driver of AB3 (Transparency). Public-records law is a transparency fact. B3 asks a different question: does the entity tell the people it serves what it can and cannot do? Michigan fails B3 on its own service evidence, the Michigan 211 material condition finding, and that is now the load-bearing citation. FOIA remains as context, not as the primary fact.

B5 was scored on an assessor search failure rather than on Michigan conduct. The prior run gave B5 a 2 at low confidence because no consent policy had been found. One targeted search located MDHHS Administrative Policy API 181 (effective 1 October 2025) and the statutory Mental Health Code recipient-rights framework. The evidence existed; the prior run ran out of budget before reaching it. Scoring a state down for the assessor's incomplete search is a methodology violation. B5 corrects to 3.
bnd_correction.note: B3 remains 2. The FOIA finding itself is verified and is more severe for Michigan than the prior framing suggested; the correction is about which subdimension carries it, not about softening it.

struck_factual_error.claim_struck: Four Michigan prisoner deaths were investigated as homicides.
struck_factual_error.status: Unsupported by primary reporting. Traced to a partisan source. Struck from the record.
struck_factual_error.what_is_true: Four women died at the Women's Huron Valley Correctional Facility between 13 May and 2 July 2026. This is real and verified. The released causes are a pulmonary blood clot, a suicide, and a cardiac arrest. No homicide investigation is supported by primary reporting.
struck_factual_error.scoring_effect: The four deaths still drive I3 (Internal Consistency) to 2. Removing the homicide claim changed the justification, not the score.

cross_state_consistency_flag.issue: Michigan and Massachusetts are the only two states exempting both the governor and the legislature from public-records law, but the two exemptions are not equivalent. Michigan is the only state in the country with a blanket statutory exemption for the governor and executive office staff, written into the statute. The Massachusetts exemption rests on a state high-court ruling that the public records law does not explicitly list the governor, a judicial interpretation reversible by legislation. On the methodology, Michigan's version is the more severe fact.
cross_state_consistency_flag.aggravating_detail: Michigan Senate Bills 1 and 2 were the first two bills introduced in the 2025-26 Senate and passed 33-2. House Speaker Matt Hall declared them dead the next day, calling them a very low priority. As of March 2026 they had sat in the House Government Operations Committee for over a year, and a senator was proposing a ballot measure to bypass the Legislature. Michigan is not a state that failed to get to this; it is a state where one chamber's leadership stopped it after the other chamber voted 33-2 for it.
cross_state_consistency_flag.resolution: To be resolved at Massachusetts, not by adjusting Michigan. Massachusetts is published at 94.4 from the corrupt legacy index and is not a valid anchor. If Massachusetts genuinely carries a high BND score while holding the weaker version of the same exemption, the Massachusetts score is the one that is wrong. Flag for the Massachusetts full-depth re-assessment.

reviewer_cautions.observation: Michigan generates more adversarial evidence about itself than peer states because it maintains a genuinely independent Office of the Auditor General and operates under an active federal consent decree (Dwayne B. v. Whitmer). Both produce verifiable, unflattering documentation that most states never generate. Michigan therefore looks worse than comparably performing states partly because it publishes more of its own failures.
reviewer_cautions.rationale: This assessment does not adjust for the effect. Reviewers should be alert to it as a systematic bias against transparent jurisdictions when comparing Michigan with states that have weaker oversight infrastructure.

key_evidence: Rx Kids is a $270 million state-funded unconditional cash program paying $1,500 during pregnancy and $500 per month for a child's first year. More than 13,000 families have enrolled and it expanded to the entire Upper Peninsula in March 2026. Its founder testified that infant maltreatment allegations in Flint dropped significantly since launch. This is the single most consequential item in Michigan's file and the prior degraded run missed it entirely.

The bipartisan FY27 budget delivered 3 July 2026 totals $75.2 billion and includes $200 million to continue free school meals for all 1.4 million public school students, saving families around $1,000 per child per year.

A 2026 Office of the Auditor General audit found MDHHS did not sufficiently monitor Michigan 211, which handles about 576,000 contacts annually. The referral database failed accuracy standards in six of the seven quarters reviewed, meaning residents seeking food, housing or health care could be sent to the wrong place.

Michigan is the only state with a blanket statutory public-records exemption for the governor and executive office staff. Reform bills passed the Senate 33-2 and were declared dead by the House Speaker the following day.

Four women died at the Women's Huron Valley Correctional Facility between 13 May and 2 July 2026. The facility faces two federal lawsuits, one alleging the presence of toxic mold. This drives Integrity to its lowest component score.
